commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix flashcard swipe stale closure bug

Use a ref (dragXRef) alongside state to track drag distance so that
onTouchEnd/onMouseUp always read the latest value. Previously, React's
batched rendering could cause the end handlers to see a stale dragX
from state, causing swipes near the threshold to fail silently.

fix v4.5: revert process.env — not available in n8n Code sandbox

process.env causes ReferenceError. Reverted to $env (n8n Variables).
User must add TOGETHER_API_KEY to n8n Settings → Variables (not Docker env).

commit.message:
Fix dtype mismatch: use autocast(dtype=float32) instead of enabled=False

enabled=False disables autocast entirely, so F.linear gets fp32 input
vs bf16 weight and raises RuntimeError.  autocast(dtype=float32) keeps
autocast active to auto-promote bf16 weights to fp32.

fix: rename weekPlans to weekplans for case-sensitive Linux filesystem

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add GRC data model expansion per Security Program Addendum

New objects:
- Control_Domain__c: top-level domain layer (Framework > Domain > Category > Control hierarchy)
- Evidence__c: evidence tracking linked to Controls, Audit Findings, and Client Environments

New fields on existing objects:
- Control_Category__c: Domain__c lookup to Control_Domain__c
- Control__c: Control_ID__c, Description__c, Review_Frequency__c, Evidence_Required__c, Control_Owner__c, Implementation_Guidance__c
- Audit_Finding__c: Severity__c, Evidence_Required__c, Client_Environment__c
- Risk__c: Description__c, Likelihood__c, Impact__c, Risk_Score__c (formula), Client_Environment__c, Mitigation_Plan__c

Updated all 6 profiles and SimpSec_Full_Access permission set with field and object permissions for new components.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Milestone 1.5: ARM64 port and triple-license as MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD-2-Clause

ARM64 support: BusyBox boots on QEMU virt (cortex-a72) with GICv2,
PL011 UART, generic timer, and virtio-mmio transport. All 79 syscalls
work unmodified on both x86_64 and ARM64.

License: add BSD-2-Clause as a third option alongside MIT and Apache-2.0,
aligning with FreeBSD (the primary syscall semantics reference). Updated
all SPDX headers (212 files), Cargo.toml license fields, LICENSE.md,
NOTICE, README, and documentation.

commit.message:
fix(57): recording deletion cascade via RPC (#74)

Add delete_recording RPC (SECURITY DEFINER) that safely cascades through
workspace_entries, folder_assignments, and call_tags before deleting the
recording itself. Bypasses the RLS NOT EXISTS guard that previously blocked
deletion when workspace_entries existed.

Also adds deleteRecording() service function to call the RPC from the frontend.

Closes #57

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: improve Fiskevejr visuals - bigger arrows, cleaner labels, better spacing

- Direction arrows: 32px with white outline, drop shadow, extrabold text labels
- Value pills: show only numeric value + unit, no redundant direction text
- Primary graphs: 130px height minimum for all 5 essential trends
- Secondary graphs: 90px height minimum
- Graph row spacing: mb-3 and space-y-3 for better breathing room
- Responsive TimeAxis margin matching left label column (70px/100px)

feat(58): cross-org copy RPC with permission checks + default workspace auto-entry (#73)

Adds copy_recording_to_organization() RPC that verifies caller membership
in BOTH source and target orgs before copying recording + transcript_chunks.
Adds auto_create_default_workspace_entry trigger so new recordings always
appear in the org's HOME workspace. Drops the old batch copy RPC which
only checked target org membership.

commit.message:
Bypass Unsloth model patching: use standard HF + PEFT for model loading

Unsloth 2026.3.4 Fast Qwen3 patching has a dtype bug in matmul_lora
(fp16 output buffer + bf16 LoRA) that crashes even without 4-bit quant.
Now loads model with standard AutoModelForCausalLM + PEFT LoRA while
still using Unsloth's GRPO trainer optimizations.

commit.message:
Add dual-format support: CBMFS 1581 filesystem and format detection

Implement full 1581 CBMFS filesystem (mount, read, write, delete, format)
with modular I/O abstraction for future D81 image support. Add automatic
disk format detection (FAT12-HD/DD vs CBMFS-1581) based on density sensor
and filesystem signatures, with geometry helpers for HD/DD layouts.

65 tests pass: 27 D64 + 24 CBMFS + 14 format detection.

feat: add field semantic classification for smart chart content migration

AI-driven field classification step that runs after field type inference,
categorizing each form field as demographic (skip), medical (enrich patient),
clinical (keep with meaningful key), or administrative (skip). This fixes
empty chart content by excluding demographics fields that have null values
in Boulevard's API, generating human-readable template keys instead of
opaque blvd_UUID, and enriching patient records with allergies/medical
history from forms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Rust-style error handling — return err, switch ok/err, ??, is_err/is_ok

Add structured error handling inspired by Rust's Result model:
- `return err { kind, message }` language primitive for typed errors
- `switch { ok/err }` Result routing with specific err "kind" matching
- `??` null-coalesce operator in expressions
- `is_err()`, `is_ok()`, `unwrap_or()` expression functions
- `?` propagation marker in function bodies
- Result output model: ok = raw value, err = { err: { kind, message, node } }
- Hand-written RDP parser (jwl_lexer/jwl_parser/jwl_token) as default parser
- classify_error() for automatic error kind detection

commit.message:
Force fp32 for decoder forward to prevent NaN gradients in bf16

Backward through ~28 Qwen transformer layers loses precision in bf16
(7-bit mantissa), causing NaN gradients after ~80 training steps.
Disable autocast and cast inputs to fp32 for the decoder forward in
both forward_ntp and decode_train, so backward is also fp32.
Encoder + perceiver remain in bf16 for speed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(release-groups): filter stored false positives case-insensitively in endpoint

Adds isValidReleaseGroup() in the /release-groups route that re-checks
every candidate (stored or runtime-derived) against a regex for
resolution tokens (/^\d+[pPiIkK]$/i) and an uppercase exclusion set
covering resolutions, codecs, sources, and app names. This catches
values like 1080p/2160p/720p that were stored in the DB before the
ffprobe blocklist existed and bypassed extraction-time filtering.

commit.message:
fix: suppress duplicate alert tone on foreground resume after background timer expiry

When the app returns to foreground after the rest timer expired in the
background, the RunLoop timer's tick() would fire before handleForegroundResume()
and treat it as a live expiry, playing alert_tone.caf again even though the
local notification already played it. Now tick() checks if the expiry is stale
(>2s old) and suppresses sound for background expiries.
